name: Feedback Inhibition (e.g., ATCase)
description: Feedback inhibition: the end product of a biosynthetic pathway binds an allosteric site on the first committed enzyme (e.g., ATCase), shifting it to a low-activity state and reducing flux when product is abundant.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Substrate enters pathway"]
    N2["First committed enzyme"]
    N3["Catalyze rate-limiting step"]
    N4["Intermediate metabolites"]
    N5["End product synthesized"]
    N6{"End product abundant?"}
    N7["Binds allosteric site"]
    N8["Enzyme T-state; activity down"]
    N9["Flux reduced (feedback)"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|yes| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|inhibit| N3
    N6 -->|no: flux on| N3

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

nodeDetails.label: End product abundant?
nodeDetails.detail: Is product above the set point?

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

sources.title: Evidence for a negative-feedback mechanism in the biosynthesis of isoleucine
On the nature of allosteric transitions: a plausible model
sources.authors: Monod, J.; Wyman, J.; Changeux, J. P.

sources.title: The enzymology of control by feedback inhibition
sources.authors: Gerhart, J. C.; Pardee, A. B.
